examTitle: Arts & Culture CEFR A2 Mock Exam

parts.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Reading on Museum Visits
parts.content: The museum was very busy when we visited. We had to wait for 30 minutes to get in. The museum had four floors. Each floor had many things to see. There were paintings, sculptures, and even old cars. The museum had a big cafe where we could buy food and drinks. We saw a lot of interesting things, but we wanted to see more. We decided to come back another day. This time, we wanted to see the new exhibition.
parts.instructions: Read the text and answer the questions. For each question, choose the correct answer A, B or C.

questions.question: Why was the museum very busy?
options: Because many people wanted to visit.
questions.explanation: The answer is 'Because many people wanted to visit.' This is because the text states that the museum had to wait for 30 minutes to get in, implying that there were a lot of visitors.

questions.question: How many floors did the museum have?
questions.explanation: The answer is 'Four'. This is because the text explicitly states that the museum had four floors.

questions.question: What did we buy at the cafe?
questions.explanation: The answer is 'Food and drinks'. This is because the text states that we could buy food and drinks at the cafe.

questions.question: What did we want to see more of?
questions.explanation: The answer is 'Everything'. This is because the text states that we wanted to see more, implying that we wanted to see everything that the museum had to offer.

questions.question: Why did we decide to come back another day?
options: Because we wanted to buy souvenirs.
Because we wanted to see the new exhibition.
Because we wanted to see the old cars.
questions.explanation: The answer is 'Because we wanted to see the new exhibition.' This is because the text states that we decided to come back another day to see the new exhibition.

questions.question: What did we see at the museum?
options: Many things, including paintings, sculptures, and old cars.
questions.explanation: The answer is 'Many things, including paintings, sculptures, and old cars.' This is because the text states that we saw a lot of interesting things, including paintings, sculptures, and old cars.

parts.tips: When reading the text, pay close attention to the details. Use context clues to help you answer the questions.

content.D: A young woman with a camera

options: A young woman with a camera

parts.title: Part 3: Multiple-Choice for Short Texts/Signs
content: The museum is open from 10am to 5pm.
Please do not touch the exhibits.
The cafe serves coffee, tea, and sandwiches.
The museum is closed on Mondays.
Please wear a mask when you visit.
The ticket price is 10 euros for adults and 5 euros for children.
parts.instructions: Read the texts and short messages. For each question, choose the correct answer A, B or C.

questions.question: When is the museum open?
questions.explanation: The answer is 'From 10am to 5pm'. This is because the text states that the museum is open from 10am to 5pm.

questions.question: What should you not do at the museum?
questions.explanation: The answer is 'Touch the exhibits'. This is because the text states that you should not touch the exhibits.

questions.question: What does the cafe serve?
questions.explanation: The answer is 'Coffee, tea, and sandwiches'. This is because the text states that the cafe serves coffee, tea, and sandwiches.

questions.question: When is the museum closed?
questions.explanation: The answer is 'On Mondays'. This is because the text states that the museum is closed on Mondays.

questions.question: What should you wear when visiting the museum?
questions.explanation: The answer is 'A mask'. This is because the text states that you should wear a mask when you visit.

questions.question: How much does a ticket cost?
options: 5 euros for adults and 10 euros for children
10 euros for adults and 5 euros for children
20 euros for adults and 10 euros for children
questions.explanation: The answer is '10 euros for adults and 5 euros for children'. This is because the text states that the ticket price is 10 euros for adults and 5 euros for children.

parts.tips: When reading the texts and short messages, look for key words and phrases that can help you answer the questions.

parts.title: Part 4: Multiple-Choice Gap-Fill
parts.content: The museum has a (1) _______ of art from all over the world. We saw many (2) _______ paintings, including a famous one by Van Gogh. The museum is located in a (3) _______ area of the city. It's easy to (4) _______ to by public transportation. The museum is (5) _______ open from 10am to 5pm. Visitors are asked to (6) _______ their bags before entering.
parts.instructions: Read the text. Choose the best word (A, B or C) for each gap.

questions.question: Gap 1
questions.explanation: The answer is 'a collection'. This is because the text states that the museum has a collection of art from all over the world.

questions.question: Gap 2
questions.explanation: The answer is 'many'. This is because the text states that we saw many paintings, including a famous one by Van Gogh.

questions.question: Gap 3
questions.explanation: The answer is 'central'. This is because the text states that the museum is located in a central area of the city.

questions.question: Gap 4
questions.explanation: The answer is 'get to'. This is because the text states that it's easy to get to the museum by public transportation.

questions.question: Gap 5
questions.explanation: The answer is 'not'. This is because the text states that the museum is not open from 10am to 5pm.

questions.question: Gap 6
questions.explanation: The answer is 'take'. This is because the text states that visitors are asked to take their bags before entering.

parts.tips: When filling in the gaps, look for words that fit grammatically and semantically in the sentence.

parts.title: Part 5: Open Gap-Fill
parts.content: The museum is (1) _______ a famous art collection. We saw many (2) _______ paintings, including a famous one by Van Gogh. The museum is (3) _______ in a central area of the city. It's easy to (4) _______ to by public transportation. The museum is (5) _______ open from 10am to 5pm. Visitors are asked to (6) _______ their bags before entering.
parts.instructions: Read the text. Write ONE word for each gap.

questions.question: Gap 1
questions.explanation: The answer is 'houses'. This is because the text states that the museum is housed in a famous art collection.
questions.tips: When filling in the gap, think about the context and the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: Gap 2
questions.explanation: The answer is 'many'. This is because the text states that we saw many paintings, including a famous one by Van Gogh.
questions.tips: When filling in the gap, look for words that fit grammatically and semantically in the sentence.

questions.question: Gap 3
questions.explanation: The answer is 'located'. This is because the text states that the museum is located in a central area of the city.
questions.tips: When filling in the gap, think about the context and the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: Gap 4
questions.explanation: The answer is 'get'. This is because the text states that it's easy to get to the museum by public transportation.
questions.tips: When filling in the gap, look for words that fit grammatically and semantically in the sentence.

questions.question: Gap 5
questions.explanation: The answer is 'never'. This is because the text states that the museum is never open from 10am to 5pm.
questions.tips: When filling in the gap, think about the context and the meaning of the sentence.

questions.question: Gap 6
questions.explanation: The answer is 'take'. This is because the text states that visitors are asked to take their bags before entering.
questions.tips: When filling in the gap, look for words that fit grammatically and semantically in the sentence.

parts.tips: When filling in the gaps, think about the context and the meaning of the sentence. Look for words that fit grammatically and semantically in the sentence.
